name: Transposon and Somatic Mutation Control Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which source-completeness gaps for Transposon and Somatic Mutation Control need repair, and do FTC/TAF biological-age data, RTI Alzheimer registries, TPN-101/ARPA-H activity, DREAM biology, or TET2 CHIP canakinumab trial-watch context change public boundaries?

summary: Coverage repair resolved the named source-completeness gaps by source-reviewing and staging FTC/TAF DNA methylation biological-age preprint context, LINE-AD and SANTA no-results Alzheimer/MCI RTI registries, ARPA-H and Brown TPN-101/Censavudine healthy-aging program activity, NCT06691217 TET2 CHIP canakinumab trial-watch context, the DREAM somatic-mutation mechanism paper, and a recent transposable-elements-in-aging review. The staged public update keeps the track at low-confidence human_biomarker_signal with steady momentum: the new sources improve completeness and branch boundaries, but do not supply controlled human target-engagement, functional, durable disease, clone-size, mutation-burden, or mortality evidence.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repair changes the public evidence map and source list but not the public stage, confidence, momentum, blockers, or rating-change criteria direction. Public staging is warranted because the missing sources materially clarify RTI human biomarker context, active RTI and CHIP trial-watch records, TPN-101 aging-program activity, DREAM somatic-mutation mechanism, and recent review triangulation.

search_log.summary: Confirmed PMID 41929344 as a medRxiv preprint on FTC/TAF and DNA methylation aging in healthy adults, PMID 42230995 as a Nature Aging DREAM/somatic-mutation paper, and PMID 42219647 as a 2026 Advances in Genetics review chapter on transposable elements in aging.

Confirmed LINE-AD and SANTA as recruiting no-results placebo-controlled emtricitabine or FTC/TAF Alzheimer/MCI trial-watch records. LINE-AD lists estimated enrollment 35 and safety, inflammatory, cognitive, functional, and CSF biomarker endpoints; SANTA lists estimated enrollment 48 and safety, Alzheimer biomarker, inflammatory, cognitive, and ADL endpoints.

Confirmed NCT06691217 as a recruiting no-results phase 2 randomized canakinumab trial in coronary artery disease with and without TET2 CHIP, including vascular inflammation, cytokine, and TET2 variant-allele-fraction endpoints.

search_log.query: TPN-101 OR censavudine and Transposon Therapeutics aging censavudine
search_log.summary: Found TPN-101 disease-context registries in AGS, ALS/FTD, and PSP, but no ClinicalTrials.gov registry for the ARPA-H healthy-aging program during this pass.

search_log.source_name: ARPA-H, Brown University, and Transposon Therapeutics web sources
search_log.query: ARPA-H award 4201, Brown aging research ARPA, and Transposon Therapeutics TPN-101 healthy aging release
search_log.summary: Confirmed official ARPA-H award context and Brown program details for a TPN-101/Censavudine healthy-aging program, including planned healthy older adult clinical testing; treated the Transposon Therapeutics press release as corroborating sponsor context rather than a primary scientific source.

excluded_sources.title: Transposon Therapeutics' TPN-101 to be Studied for Extending Healthy Aging under ARPA-H Award for up to $22M
excluded_sources.reason: Sponsor release corroborates TPN-101 program intent and LINE1 positioning but is lower-independence program context than ARPA-H and Brown sources; it is logged but not staged as a separate evidence source.

excluded_sources.title: A Phase 2a Study of TPN-101 in Patients With Progressive Supranuclear Palsy (PSP)
excluded_sources.reason: TPN-101 disease-context registry is completed without posted results and is not the healthy-aging ARPA-H program named in the coverage gap.

excluded_sources.title: A Phase 2a Study of TPN-101 in Patients With C9ORF72 ALS/FTD
excluded_sources.reason: TPN-101 disease-context registry is completed without posted results and is not the healthy-aging ARPA-H program named in the coverage gap.

excluded_sources.title: TPN-101 in Aicardi-Goutieres Syndrome
excluded_sources.reason: TPN-101 AGS registry is disease-context and terminated, not healthy-aging evidence for this repair.

notes: The repair treats PMID 41929344 as a bounded human biomarker signal because it is a preprint post hoc DNAm analysis without direct retrotransposon readouts.
The repair treats LINE-AD, SANTA, and NCT06691217 as trial-watch context only until results are posted.
The repair treats TPN-101/ARPA-H as program momentum, not clinical evidence.
The repair keeps DREAM, LINE1/NRTI, CHIP/canakinumab, and TE-review branches separate in the staged outlook.
next_actions: Run evidence reviews for source fidelity, interpretation/forecast, safety limitations, and taxonomy mapping.
If reviews approve, publish the staged repair bundle and then resume ordinary surveillance.
